title: carex acutiformis
intro: Carex acutiformis, also known as the lesser tussock sedge, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Cyperaceae, commonly found in wetlands and marshy areas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Carex acutiformis include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Carex acutiformis can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support lung health and relieve symptoms of colds and flu.
